channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew readies for Monday's lunar flyby. Here's what you need to know
item.description: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.
item.content:encoded:
At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: UCLA storms past South Carolina to claim its 1st NCAA women's basketball title
item.description: UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.
item.content:encoded:
UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.

(Image credit: Rick Scuteri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
item.description: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.
item.content:encoded:
The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

(Image credit: Hector Retamal)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Hollywood studios reach a tentative agreement with writers union
item.description: The Writers Guild of America went on strike for months in 2023 in a dispute with Hollywood studios. This year the union announced a new four-year contract after just a few weeks of negotiations.
item.content:encoded:
The Writers Guild of America went on strike for months in 2023 in a dispute with Hollywood studios. This year the union announced a new four-year contract after just a few weeks of negotiations.

(Image credit: Valerie Macon)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: These rock-climbing fish can shimmy up a 50-foot waterfall
item.description: New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.
item.content:encoded:
New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.

(Image credit: Pacifique Kiwele Mutambala)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted
item.description: Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.
item.content:encoded:
Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Questions to help you get 'financially naked' with your partner
item.description: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.content:encoded: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
item.description: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.
item.content:encoded:
In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: German researchers set right the story of a 9,000-year-old shaman's grave
item.description: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.content:encoded: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz
